title: Sony faces strong backlash for its bet on digital format: players, companies, and GitHub defend physical media
summary: Discontent over the elimination of physical games is reflected in surveys, purchase restrictions for disc drives, and threats from companies to stop producing physical copies.

title: Artificial intelligence advances amid technical and ethical debates and its integration into society
summary: While OpenAI faces criticism over GPT-5.5 performance, Google promotes AI in a historic commercial, and journalists analyze Sam Altman's quasi-religious influence.

title: Multiple security flaws and scams put user and business privacy at risk
summary: From vulnerabilities in Apple services to phishing campaigns and data extortion, cybersecurity remains a critical challenge.

title: New laptops, monitors, and components boost gaming performance
summary: System76 launches a laptop with RTX 5070, OLED monitors become affordable, magnetic keyboards gain ground, and Intel prepares its Nova Lake CPUs to compete with AMD.

title: Heat waves and energy crisis drive new climate and communication solutions
summary: From the removal of energy efficiency pages in the U.S. to testing Wi-Fi in fire hydrants in Japan, the world seeks to adapt to extreme heat and resource scarcity.

title: Anthropic's Claude and Fable star in AI tool and development news
summary: From a tool written almost entirely by Claude to software that reduces token costs and Alibaba's ban on its use, the Anthropic ecosystem is at the center of current events.

title: Digital platforms adjust their models: YouTube mobilizes creators, Plex raises prices, and CircleChat arrives
summary: YouTube faces regulatory changes in the UK, Plex drastically increases the cost of its lifetime pass, and a new messaging app, CircleChat, enters the market.

title: Scientific discoveries: jellyfish regeneration and the origin of the Egyptian pyramids
summary: Scientists investigate the ability of jellyfish to heal wounds in minutes, while archaeologists find new clues about the construction of the pyramids in ancient tombs.

title: Apple and Meta face criticism for their wearable plans: cancellations and subscriptions
summary: Apple has reportedly abandoned its camera-equipped AirPods Pro, while Meta imposes a monthly payment for key features on its smart glasses.

title: Creator of Windows Task Manager uses heat from an AMD Threadripper to power a Stirling engine
summary: Dave Plummer demonstrates how waste heat from a high-performance processor can run a 19th-century engine, offering an auxiliary cooling solution.

title: Sony developed a DualShock controller with an integrated PlayStation, but the project was canceled
summary: The device, known as PlayStation Puga, offered a complete console inside the controller, but Sony scrapped it before launching it on the market.

title: Midjourney demands Hollywood studios reveal how they use artificial intelligence
summary: The AI image generation company asks for transparency about the use of its tools in the film industry.

title: Meta suspends data center discharges for contaminating Cheyenne's water supply with bacteria
summary: Water discharges from the data center's cooling system spread metal-resistant bacteria, forcing system cleanup and suspension of operations for months.

title: Memory price increases moderate although AI demand maintains upward trend
summary: Consumers reach the affordability limit, but demand for artificial intelligence continues to raise DRAM and NAND prices until 2026.

title: Lenovo incorporates SSD drives from Chinese manufacturer YMTC in its laptops
summary: YMTC SSDs, a Chinese NAND manufacturer, arrive in Lenovo computers, although early reviews indicate below-average performance.

title: Startup presents first 3D-printed thorium nuclear reactor to power AI data centers
summary: The factory-built, solid-state, subcritical reactor promises clean, constant power for the growing demand of artificial intelligence data centers.

title: Cyberpunk 2077 surpasses 40 million copies while gaming classics are remembered
summary: CD Projekt Red's game reaches a sales milestone, while reflection is made on the most important titles in video game history.

title: Heat waves cause blackouts in the U.S. and threaten the flow of the Rhine in Europe
summary: While nearly a million people lose power in the United States, the low level of the Rhine River causes logistical and energy problems in Europe.

title: Problems with 12VHPWR cables and high RTX 5090 prices mark current GPU news
summary: While users must take extreme precautions with power connectors, the new RTX 5090 reaches record prices in French stores.

title: Gaming monitor reviews: Xiaomi Mini LED and AOC Dual-Refresh offer high quality
summary: Two reviews detail the performance and price-quality ratio of these monitors, highlighting the speed and flexibility of the AOC and the Mini LED technology of the Xiaomi.
